---
name: 🔥 New Scenario Request
about: Suggest a new real-world cloud engineering scenario to add to Tech Passport
title: '[SCENARIO] '
labels: new-scenario, community
assignees: ausjones84
---

## Scenario Name
What would you call this scenario? (e.g., "Multi-Region RDS Failover")

## Cloud Platform
- [ ] AWS
- [ ] Google Cloud (GCP)
- [ ] Microsoft Azure
- [ ] Multi-cloud
- [ ] Kubernetes / Container (platform-agnostic)
- [ ] Other: ___

## Difficulty Level
- [ ] Beginner
- [ ] Intermediate
- [ ] Advanced

## The Real-World Problem
Describe the real-world production incident or engineering challenge this scenario would simulate.

**Example:** "A production RDS database fails over to a secondary region but the app is still pointing at the old endpoint. Engineers need to diagnose the connectivity issue, update connection strings, and restore service."

## Why Is This Important?
Why would cloud engineers actually face this in a real job or interview?

## Key Skills Tested
What skills or knowledge would this scenario help engineers practice?
- Skill 1
- Skill 2
- Skill 3

## Architecture Involved
What cloud components/services are involved? (e.g., EC2, RDS, Route53, ALB, ECS, etc.)

## Have You Faced This Scenario?
- [ ] Yes, in production
- [ ] Yes, in an interview
- [ ] No, but I've seen it discussed
- [ ] No, just think it would be valuable

## Additional Context
Any links, documentation, blog posts, or post-mortems that relate to this scenario?

---
> Real scenarios requested by real engineers make Tech Passport genuinely valuable. Thank you! 🔥
